---
title: The Body Keeps the Score
author: Bessel van der Kolk
date: '2025-10-31'
categories:
- Psychology
- Book
image: cover.jpg
subtitle: ★★★★ | Brain, Mind, and Body in the Healing of Trauma
rating: 4
body-class: project-detail-view
include-before-body:
  text: '<div style=''margin-top: 50px;''></div>'
---


<style>
  #quarto-margin-sidebar {
    padding-top: 50px;
  }
</style>

# The Body Keeps the Score
Key points: 
- The mere opportunity to escape does not necessarily make traumatised animals or people take the road to freedom
- Traumatised people keep secreting large amounts of stress hormones long after the actual danger has passed
- Reenactments were an unconscious attempt that they could eventually lead to mastery and resolution
- For traumatised people, re-exposure to stress might provide a similar relief from anxiety as morphine
- The brain is formed in a use dependent manner, describing neuroplasticity. When a circuit fires repeatedly, become a default setting - the response most likely to occur. If you feel safe and loved, your brain becomes specialized in exploration, play, and cooperation; if you are frightened and unwanted, it specializes in managing feelings of fear and abandonment.

## Reading Journey

- 2025-10-31: Started
